importance: Apple is a practical daily fruit for fiber, hydration support, and appetite control.
cons: can be overused without protein pairing
portionTips: 1 medium apple is a good serving
pair with yogurt or nuts for better meal balance
cautions: Juice form removes much of the fiber benefit

importance: Orange is a common vitamin C rich fruit useful for immunity and meal freshness.
cons: higher sugar if consumed in large quantities
portionTips: 1 medium orange per serving
prefer whole fruit over juice
cautions: Watch portions in strict low-carb phases

importance: Cooked rice is a globally common carb source and ideal for energy and recovery meals.
cons: can overshoot calories if portions are untracked
portionTips: 120-250 g cooked depending on activity
add vegetables and protein for balance

importance: Egg is a dense whole-food protein source with essential amino acids and choline, useful for muscle repair and brain function.
cons: higher cholesterol than many protein options
fried versions can add excess fat
portionTips: 2-3 whole eggs in a meal for active adults
Add vegetables for better micronutrient density
cautions: If you have specific cholesterol-related medical advice, follow your clinician guidance

importance: Chicken breast is a lean, high-protein food that helps preserve and build muscle while keeping calories controlled.
cons: processed variants can be high sodium
portionTips: 100-180 g cooked per meal depending on goal
Pair with carbs around intense training
cautions: Avoid over-frying and excess sodium-heavy marinades

importance: Oats provide slow-digesting carbohydrates and soluble fiber, supporting stable energy and digestive health.
cons: instant sweetened oats can add sugar
may feel heavy before very intense sessions
portionTips: 40-80 g dry oats per meal
Increase water and protein add-ons for balance
cautions: Watch added sugar in flavored oats

importance: Wheat flour is a staple energy source and base for roti/chapati, supporting daily carbohydrate needs.
benefits: can be fiber-rich when whole wheat
portionTips: 2-4 rotis depending on activity level
Combine with protein and vegetables
cautions: Refined flour-heavy diets can lower fiber quality

importance: Banana offers practical carbs and potassium, making it a convenient pre-workout and recovery fruit.
portionTips: 1 medium banana pre-session
Pair with protein if used as meal component
cautions: If calorie targets are strict, count fruit portions

importance: Milk provides protein, calcium, and fluid in one food, useful for recovery and daily nutrition quality.
portionTips: 200-300 ml serving
Use low-fat/full-fat based on calorie target

importance: Yogurt supports protein intake and digestive balance, especially useful with high-protein diets.
cons: sweetened forms can be high sugar
portionTips: 150-250 g per serving
Choose unsweetened options more often

importance: Ghee is a concentrated cooking fat useful for flavor and energy, but portion control matters due to high calories.
portionTips: 1-2 tsp in most meals is usually enough
Track total fat intake for the day
cautions: High calorie density; easy to overconsume
pairingIdeas: small ghee amount in rice meals

importance: Olive oil provides predominantly monounsaturated fats and works well for balanced meal quality.
cons: not ideal for all high-heat cooking
portionTips: 1-2 tbsp per meal context
Use as part of total daily fat budget

importance: Potato is a common starch source that supports training energy and satiety when cooked with minimal added fat.
portionTips: 150-300 g cooked depending on training load

importance: Lentils are a very common plant protein and fiber staple for long-term health and satiety.
cons: some people need gradual intake for digestion comfort
portionTips: 120-200 g cooked per meal as protein side
cautions: Soak and cook properly for better digestion

importance: Chickpeas are a common legume staple providing fiber and moderate protein.
cons: can cause bloating if increased too quickly
portionTips: 100-180 g cooked in balanced meals
cautions: Rinse canned chickpeas to reduce sodium

importance: Whole wheat bread is a common convenience carb with better fiber than refined white bread.
portionTips: 2-4 slices based on calorie target
cautions: Check ingredient labels for added sugar
